channel.title: José Silva - Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on José Silva - Notes

item.title: Friction-Cone Discretisation
item.description: Summary The Coulomb friction model defines a convex cone of admissible contact forces; solving contact-rich QPs requires discretising this cone into either a linear pyramid approximation or an exact second-order cone.

item.title: HZD and Virtual Constraints
item.description: Summary Hybrid Zero Dynamics (HZD) provides the first formal stability proofs for periodic bipedal walking by reducing the full hybrid system to a low-dimensional zero-dynamics manifold and checking stability of the resulting Poincaré return map.

item.title: Observability of the Pseudo-Measurement and EKF Convergence
item.description: Summary The contact wrench pseudo-measurement \hat{W} = (J(q)^\top)^+ (M(q)\ddot{q} + h(q,\dot{q}) - S^\top \tau_{\text{cmd}}) loses rank at kinematic singularities, making certain force directions unobservable to the EKF.

item.title: Real-Time Digital Twin Synchronisation
item.description: Summary A digital twin (DT) running alongside a real-time controller is only as useful as the tightness of its synchronisation with the physical robot; non-RT kernels and default DDS configurations introduce jitter that silently lags the DT state by one or more control periods.

item.title: Welcome
item.description: To my vault. Here I store my notes from my MSc, projects I’m up to, research-related notes.

item.title: Notes
item.description: Research notes on embedded systems, robotics, and control theory. Distilled from papers, books, and implementation work.
